binaries.title: ntfs.sys - Feature-gated bounds/size validation in $LogFile restart-table recovery made unconditional (CWE-787)

changed_functions.name: PageUpdateAnalysis
key_changes: In the restart-table copy loop, the bounds check cmp ecx,[rdi+0xc] / jnb (index vs destination capacity) was reached only when EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2076398905_59354624) returned non-zero: the test eax,eax / jz at 0x1C020606F-0x1C0206071 skipped the check when the feature evaluated to zero, and the 8-byte write at 0x1C0206083 (mov [rdi+rcx*8+0x20], rax) then used an unchecked index.
Patch removes the lea g_Feature / call EvaluateCurrentState / test eax,eax / jz sequence; the cmp ecx,[rdi+0xc] / jnb bounds check now executes unconditionally before the write.
A second feature gate on g_Feature_2859161914_59426036 in this function is present in both builds and is unchanged.
Loop counter register changed ebx->r8d and WPP trace-line immediates shifted (0x1E1758/0x1E17CD -> 0x1E17AB/0x1E1739); those are not behavioral.
changed_functions.security_notes: The gate is a WIL feature-staging flag (EvaluateCurrentState -> EvaluateFeature), evaluated at runtime on retail builds, NOT a checked-build (#if DBG) assertion. This function is part of the NTFS $LogFile restart/recovery path, not $ATTRIBUTE_LIST processing. It copies extent entries from a source restart-table record (r15, from [rsi+0x48]) into a destination restart-table entry (rdi) allocated by NtfsAllocateRestartTableIndex. The write index ecx = [r15+0x18] - [rdi+0x10] + loop_counter derives from on-disk log-record fields, and is checked against the destination capacity at [rdi+0x0c]. Removing the feature gate makes the bounds check unconditional. Whether the unpatched retail default left the check disabled, and whether an out-of-range index can survive the upstream log-record validators, were not determined from the binaries, so no reachable overflow is demonstrated.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (0x1C0206058-0x1C0206083):
0x1C0206058: jnb loc_1C0206093              ; loop exit
0x1C020605A: lea rcx, g_Feature_2076398905_59354624_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C0206061: call EvaluateCurrentState     ; runtime feature evaluation
0x1C0206066: mov ecx, [r15+18h]            ; src VCN field (on-disk log record)
0x1C020606A: sub ecx, [rdi+10h]            ; minus dest start VCN
0x1C020606D: add ecx, ebx                  ; plus loop counter -> write index
0x1C020606F: test eax, eax                 ; feature result
0x1C0206071: jz loc_1C020607C              ; feature == 0 -> skip bounds check
0x1C0206073: cmp ecx, [rdi+0Ch]           ; index vs capacity
0x1C0206076: jnb loc_1C020612E            ; STATUS_DISK_CORRUPT
0x1C020607C: mov eax, ebx
0x1C020607E: mov rax, [r15+rax*8+20h]     ; read source extent
0x1C0206083: mov [rdi+rcx*8+20h], rax     ; 8-byte write at computed index

PATCHED (0x1C0205FA6-0x1C0205FCB): lea/call/test/jz removed; cmp ecx,[r9+0Ch] / jnb runs unconditionally before the write.

changed_functions.name: ReadRestartTable
key_changes: The size-consistency check cmp edi,[r15+4] / jnz (computed size (count<<3)+0x20 vs declared size) was gated behind EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2076398905_59354624) via the test eax,eax / jz at 0x1C0206401-0x1C0206403 in the unpatched build. The patch removes the gate and makes the comparison unconditional.

When the check did not run, an inconsistent size was not raised as corruption and the function proceeded to call the validator NtfsCheckRestartTable (0x1C01BC394) with length [rsi+0x40]-declared_size.
NtfsCheckRestartTable is a restart-table validation routine, not a memory-copy primitive.
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-gated size-consistency validation in NTFS $LogFile restart-table processing. The computed size edi=(entry_count<<3)+0x20 (from [r15+0x0e]) is compared to the declared size [r15+0x04]. In the unpatched build the comparison was gated behind the same WIL feature flag; the patch makes it unconditional. The downstream consumer NtfsCheckRestartTable is a validator, so the effect of a bypassed check is a mis-validated restart table rather than a demonstrated out-of-bounds copy.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED: computed size in edi, then lea rcx,g_Feature_2076398905_59354624 / call EvaluateCurrentState / test eax,eax / jz skips movzx eax,[r15+4] / cmp edi,eax / jnz.
PATCHED (0x1C0206315-0x1C020633E): ... lea edi,ds:20h[rax*8] ... movzx ecx,[r8+4] / cmp edi,ecx / jnz <corrupt> with no EvaluateCurrentState gate.

changed_functions.name: InitializeRestartState
key_changes: All three EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2076398905_59354624) gates (0x1C016DF20, 0x1C016E01B, 0x1C016E11D) removed and their guarded paths made unconditional.

At the restart-table copy site around 0x1C016E01B: when the feature evaluated non-zero the copy size was computed with the overflow-checked RtlULongMult((count-1),8) before the bound compare and the memmove at 0x1C016E099; when it evaluated zero the size used a plain shl rcx,3 with no overflow detection. The patch makes the RtlULongMult path unconditional.
Stack frame reduced 0x490->0x480 and registers reallocated; memmove call count unchanged (both builds copy at the same sites).
changed_functions.security_notes: Top-level restart-state setup function that calls ReadRestartTable and copies restart-table entries with memmove. The removed feature gates guarded overflow-checked size computation (RtlULongMult) and size compares ahead of the memmove copies. Making them unconditional removes the possibility of the non-overflow-checked shl path being selected. Reached via NtfsRestartVolume during $LogFile recovery.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (0x1C016E01B-0x1C016E099): lea rcx,g_Feature_2076398905_59354624 / call EvaluateCurrentState / test eax,eax / jz to a shl rcx,3 path; the feature-on branch calls RtlULongMult and checks js/jb before memmove.
PATCHED: all three EvaluateCurrentState gates removed; overflow-checked path and compares run unconditionally.

security_findings.entry_point: Crafted NTFS volume (VHD/USB) whose $LogFile restart area is parsed during volume mount / log recovery
call_chain: Mount crafted NTFS volume; NTFS performs $LogFile restart/recovery
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Out-of-bounds write bounds check gated behind a runtime feature-staging flag; made unconditional by the patch
security_findings.description: In the unpatched ntfs.sys, PageUpdateAnalysis copies extent entries from a source restart-table record (r15, from [rsi+0x48]) into a destination restart-table entry (rdi). The write index ecx = [r15+0x18] - [rdi+0x10] + loop_counter derives from on-disk $LogFile record fields. The bounds check cmp ecx,[rdi+0xc] / jnb (index vs capacity) exists in both builds but is reached in the unpatched build only when EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2076398905_59354624) returns non-zero; the test eax,eax / jz at 0x1C020606F-0x1C0206071 skips it when the feature evaluates to zero, and the 8-byte write at 0x1C0206083 then uses an unchecked index. The patch removes the feature gate so the bounds check always guards the write. The gate is a WIL feature-staging flag evaluated at runtime on retail builds, not a checked-build assertion.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. Create a crafted NTFS volume whose $LogFile restart tables contain manipulated fields.
2. In a restart-table record set the source VCN field ([record+0x18]) and entry count ([record+0x0e]) so that (source_vcn - dest_start_vcn + counter) exceeds the destination capacity ([dest+0x0c]).
3. Mount the volume; NTFS log recovery reaches NtfsRestartVolume -> AnalysisPass -> PageUpdateAnalysis.
4. If the feature-gated bounds check does not execute, the copy loop writes past the destination allocation.
security_findings.primitive: Guarded operation is an 8-byte write into the destination restart-table entry's extent array at an index derived from on-disk fields. If the gated check is bypassed and a crafted record yields an out-of-range index, this is a controllable kernel pool write (CWE-787). A pool overflow was not reproduced against the shipped default configuration, and the runtime default state of the feature flag was not determined from the binaries.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED PageUpdateAnalysis copy loop in ntfs_unpatched.sys:
0x1C0206051: mov ebx, esi
0x1C0206053: cmp si, [r15+0Eh]                 ; counter vs entry count
0x1C0206058: jnb loc_1C0206093                 ; loop exit
0x1C020605A: lea rcx, g_Feature_2076398905_59354624_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C0206061: call EvaluateCurrentState         ; runtime feature evaluation
0x1C0206066: mov ecx, [r15+18h]                ; src VCN field
0x1C020606A: sub ecx, [rdi+10h]                ; minus dest start VCN
0x1C020606D: add ecx, ebx                       ; plus loop counter -> write index
0x1C020606F: test eax, eax
0x1C0206071: jz loc_1C020607C                  ; feature == 0 -> skip bounds check
0x1C0206073: cmp ecx, [rdi+0Ch]               ; index vs capacity
0x1C0206076: jnb loc_1C020612E                 ; STATUS_DISK_CORRUPT
0x1C020607C: mov eax, ebx
0x1C020607E: mov rax, [r15+rax*8+20h]          ; read source extent
0x1C0206083: mov [rdi+rcx*8+20h], rax          ; 8-byte write at computed index
0x1C0206088: inc ebx
0x1C020608A: movzx eax, word ptr [r15+0Eh]
0x1C020608F: cmp ebx, eax
0x1C0206091: jmp loc_1C0206058

Error path (0x1C020612E): mov dword [r14+1A0h],46h ; mov rbx,116001E17CDh ; call NtfsAttachCorruptionSimple / NtfsAttachRepairInfoPriv ; NtfsRaiseStatusInternal(STATUS_DISK_CORRUPT 0xC0000032).

PATCHED PageUpdateAnalysis copy loop in ntfs_patched.sys (0x1C0205D04):
0x1C0205F9F: cmp r14w, [r13+0Eh]
0x1C0205FA4: jnb loc_1C0205FDD
0x1C0205FA6: mov r9, [rsp+188h+var_148]
0x1C0205FAB: mov ecx, [r13+18h]
0x1C0205FAF: sub ecx, [r9+10h]
0x1C0205FB3: add ecx, r8d
0x1C0205FB6: cmp ecx, [r9+0Ch]                 ; unconditional bounds check
0x1C0205FBA: jnb loc_1C0205FFE
0x1C0205FC1: mov rcx, [r13+rax*8+20h]
0x1C0205FCB: mov [rax+rdx*8+20h], rcx           ; write at checked index

breakpoints.why: Function entry. r15 (loaded at 0x1C0205DF8 from [rsp+0x1b0]) is the source restart-table record; rdi is the destination restart-table entry.
The feature-gate call: EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2076398905_59354624). The eax result determines whether the bounds check at 0x1C0206073 is reached.
The 8-byte write mov [rdi+rcx*8+0x20], rax. Compare rcx (computed index) against [rdi+0xc] (capacity).

watch: ecx at 0x1C020606D: computed write index = [r15+0x18] - [rdi+0x10] + loop_counter
[r15+0x18] at 0x1C0206066: source VCN field from on-disk log record
eax at 0x1C020606F: EvaluateCurrentState result gating the bounds check
key_offsets: 0x1C0206061: call EvaluateCurrentState - feature gate (removed in patch)
0x1C020606F-0x1C0206071: test eax,eax / jz - skips the bounds check when feature==0 (removed in patch)
0x1C0206073: cmp ecx,[rdi+0xc] - bounds check (unconditional in patch)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Craft an NTFS volume whose $LogFile restart tables contain a restart-table record with a source VCN field ([record+0x18]) and entry count ([record+0x0e]) chosen so that (source_vcn - dest_start_vcn + counter) exceeds the destination capacity ([dest+0x0c]). Mount the volume to trigger log recovery (NtfsRestartVolume -> AnalysisPass -> PageUpdateAnalysis).
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On the patched build, an inconsistent index raises STATUS_DISK_CORRUPT (0xC0000032) at the unconditional cmp/jnb. On the unpatched build, whether that check runs depends on the runtime state of g_Feature_2076398905_59354624; if it does not run, the copy proceeds with the unchecked index.
debugging_aids.notes: This is NTFS transaction-log ($LogFile) restart/recovery, reached during volume mount, not $ATTRIBUTE_LIST attribute parsing. Restart-table entry fields referenced: capacity at +0x0c, start VCN at +0x10, source VCN field at +0x18, entry count at +0x0e, extent array at +0x20. The gate g_Feature_2076398905_59354624 is a WIL feature-staging flag evaluated via EvaluateCurrentState -> EvaluateFeature; it is referenced 5 times across the three changed functions in the unpatched build and 0 times in the patched build. Image base 0x1C000000 is the preferred load address; compute actual addresses from the loaded module base.

security_findings.entry_point: Crafted NTFS volume whose $LogFile restart record has inconsistent size / entry-count fields
call_chain: NtfsCheckRestartTable (0x1C01BC394) called with length derived from declared size
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Feature-gated size-consistency check made unconditional by the patch
security_findings.description: ReadRestartTable computes an expected restart-table size edi=(entry_count<<3)+0x20 (0x28 when entry_count<=1) from [r15+0x0e] and compares it to the declared size [r15+0x04]. In the unpatched build the comparison cmp edi,[r15+4] / jnz is gated behind EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2076398905_59354624) (test eax,eax / jz at 0x1C0206401-0x1C0206403). When the feature evaluates to zero the mismatch is not raised as corruption and the function calls NtfsCheckRestartTable with length [rsi+0x40]-declared_size. The patch makes the comparison unconditional. NtfsCheckRestartTable is a restart-table validator, not a copy primitive, so a bypassed check yields a mis-validated table rather than a demonstrated out-of-bounds copy.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. Craft a $LogFile restart record where the declared size [record+0x04] does not match (entry_count<<3)+0x20.
2. Set entry count [record+0x0e] > 1.
3. Mount and let recovery reach InitializeRestartState -> ReadRestartTable.
security_findings.primitive: Bypassed size-consistency check on a restart record; the downstream consumer is a validator, so no out-of-bounds copy is demonstrated.
security_findings.evidence:
// UNPATCHED ReadRestartTable:
0x1C02063E7 : edi = (entry_count << 3) + 0x20        // computed size, from [r15+0x0e]
0x1C02063FC : EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2076398905_59354624)
0x1C0206401 : test eax,eax ; jz -> skip size check when feature==0
0x1C020640A : cmp edi, [r15+4] ; jnz -> STATUS_DISK_CORRUPT    // gated in unpatched
0x1C0206428 : NtfsCheckRestartTable(r15+edi, [rsi+0x40]-[r15+4], ...)

// PATCHED ReadRestartTable (0x1C020633C): cmp edi,[r8+4] / jnz runs with no feature gate.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED ReadRestartTable key sequence:
0x1C02063D3: movzx eax, word ptr [r15+0Eh]       ; entry count
0x1C02063E7: lea edi, ds:20h[rax*8]              ; computed = (count<<3)+0x20
0x1C02063F0: mov edi, 28h                         ; default when count<=1
0x1C02063F5: lea rcx, g_Feature_2076398905_59354624_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C02063FC: call EvaluateCurrentState
0x1C0206401: test eax, eax
0x1C0206403: jz loc_1C0206412                     ; feature == 0 -> skip size check
0x1C0206405: movzx eax, word ptr [r15+4]          ; declared size
0x1C020640A: cmp edi, eax
0x1C020640C: jnz loc_1C02064CD                     ; STATUS_DISK_CORRUPT
0x1C0206420: sub edx, eax                          ; length = [rsi+40h] - declared size
0x1C0206428: call NtfsCheckRestartTable

PATCHED ReadRestartTable (0x1C0206294): movzx ecx,[r8+4] / cmp edi,ecx / jnz with no EvaluateCurrentState gate.

breakpoints.why: Function entry. r15 (from [r9+0x48]) points to the restart record; inspect [r15+0x0e] (entry count) and [r15+0x04] (declared size).

key_offsets: 0x1C02063D3: movzx eax, word [r15+0xe] - reads entry count
0x1C02063F5: lea rcx, g_Feature_2076398905_59354624 - feature descriptor (removed in patch)
0x1C020640A: cmp edi, [r15+4] - size-consistency check (unconditional in patch)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Craft a $LogFile restart record where (entry_count<<3)+0x20 differs from the declared size at [record+0x04]. Mount and access to trigger recovery.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On the patched build a mismatch raises STATUS_DISK_CORRUPT unconditionally; on the unpatched build it is raised only when the feature evaluates non-zero.
debugging_aids.notes: Secondary finding in the same $LogFile restart-recovery pipeline. NtfsCheckRestartTable is a validation routine, so the practical effect of a bypassed check is a mis-validated restart table.

security_findings.vulnerability_class: Feature-gated integer-overflow-checked size computation before restart-table memmove, made unconditional by the patch
security_findings.description: InitializeRestartState sets up restart state and copies restart-table entries with memmove. It has three EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2076398905_59354624) gates (0x1C016DF20, 0x1C016E01B, 0x1C016E11D). At the copy site around 0x1C016E01B, when the feature evaluates non-zero the copy size is computed with the overflow-checked RtlULongMult((count-1),8) before the bound compare and the memmove at 0x1C016E099; when it evaluates zero the size is computed with a plain shl rcx,3 that performs no overflow detection. The patch removes all three gates so the overflow-checked path and its compares run unconditionally.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. Craft a $LogFile restart table whose entry count [entry+0x0c] is large enough that (count-1)*8 stresses the size computation.
2. Mount and let recovery reach NtfsRestartVolume -> InitializeRestartState.
security_findings.primitive: Guarded operation is a restart-table memmove sized from the entry count. The removed gate could select a non-overflow-checked size computation; making RtlULongMult unconditional removes that path. No overflow was reproduced.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED InitializeRestartState gated copy path:
0x1C016E01B: lea rcx, g_Feature_2076398905_59354624_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C016E022: call EvaluateCurrentState
0x1C016E02A: mov r10d, [rbx+0Ch]                ; entry count
0x1C016E02E: lea ecx, [r10-1]
0x1C016E032: test eax, eax
0x1C016E034: jz loc_1C016E06F                    ; feature == 0 -> plain shl path
0x1C016E03E: lea edx, [r11+8]
0x1C016E042: call RtlULongMult                   ; overflow-checked (count-1)*8
0x1C016E049: js loc_1C016E9E6                     ; overflow -> error
0x1C016E064: cmp rcx, rax
0x1C016E067: jb loc_1C016E9E6
0x1C016E06F: shl rcx, 3                            ; no overflow check
0x1C016E081: cmp rdx, rcx
0x1C016E084: jb loc_1C016EA64
0x1C016E099: call memmove

PATCHED: the three EvaluateCurrentState gates at 0x1C016DF20/0x1C016E01B/0x1C016E11D are removed; the overflow-checked computation and compares run unconditionally.

breakpoints.why: Function entry into the restart-state setup path that calls ReadRestartTable and performs the restart-table memmove copies.

watch: [rbx+0x0c] at 0x1C016E02A: restart-table entry count feeding the copy size
eax at 0x1C016E032: EvaluateCurrentState result selecting the overflow-checked vs plain size path
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Craft a $LogFile restart table with a large entry count at [entry+0x0c] and mount to trigger recovery.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On the patched build the overflow-checked RtlULongMult path and size compares always run before the memmove; on the unpatched build the plain shl path could be selected when the feature evaluates to zero.
debugging_aids.notes: Same $LogFile restart-recovery pipeline. memmove call count is unchanged between builds; the change is which size-validation path is taken.

summary: The patch modifies three functions in the NTFS transaction-log ($LogFile) restart/recovery path - PageUpdateAnalysis, ReadRestartTable, and InitializeRestartState - by removing the runtime WIL feature-staging gate g_Feature_2076398905_59354624 (evaluated via EvaluateCurrentState -> EvaluateFeature) so that previously feature-gated validation runs unconditionally. In PageUpdateAnalysis this makes an 8-byte-write bounds check (index vs destination capacity) unconditional; in ReadRestartTable it makes a restart-table size-consistency check unconditional; in InitializeRestartState it makes an integer-overflow-checked size computation before a restart-table memmove unconditional. The gate is a runtime feature flag present on retail builds, not a checked-build (#if DBG) assertion, and the affected code is $LogFile recovery reached during volume mount, not $ATTRIBUTE_LIST attribute parsing. The direction is correct (the patched build is strictly stricter). The write in PageUpdateAnalysis uses an index and value derived from on-disk log-record fields, which is why the corrected CWE for that finding is CWE-787; however, no pool overflow was reproduced, the runtime default state of the feature flag in the unpatched build was not determined from the binaries, and the elaborate exploitation chain is not supported. An independent name-keyed, address-normalized diff confirmed exactly these three behavioral changes plus fourteen functions differing only in WPP tracing metadata; no functions were added or removed and no memmove/memcpy calls were added or removed.
